facts_disclosed: Thinking about starting a YouTube cooking channel — friend Fern offered to film

turns.content: have you ever watch cooking videos on YouTube na ka?? like the street food ones where they film the whole process. I have been watching SO MANY of them lately and thinking a lot 😅

Oh I've definitely fallen into that rabbit hole before! There are some amazing street food channels out there — the ones that show the whole process from start to finish are really satisfying. What's got you watching so many? Just for fun or is there something brewing?

okay so my friend Fern — she do video production, freelance — she come to eat at my cart last week and she say 'Pan, you should start a YouTube channel!' she say she can film for me. like proper filming, not just phone video. cooking tutorial style na ka

That's a cool offer! Having a friend who actually does video production is a huge advantage — most people starting YouTube are stuck with just a phone and no editing skills. What kind of tutorials would you do? Like step-by-step khao man gai recipes, or more of a behind-the-scenes street food life thing?

Fern say we should do both!! like one video 'how to make real Thai khao man gai at home' and another video 'day in the life of a street food vendor 4am to 8pm' haha. she say the 4am market run would be very popular because people love that early morning content. but honestly na ka... I am thinking about it but I don't know

Both of those are genuinely strong content ideas. The "day in my life" street food content does insanely well on YouTube — there's something about watching the pre-dawn hustle that viewers love. And a proper khao man gai tutorial from someone who sells 350 plates a day would carry way more credibility than random home cook videos.

What's making you hesitate? Is it the time commitment, or something else?

TIME na ka!! that is the thing. I already wake up 4am for the market. I cook until 2-3pm, clean the cart, go home, do my Instagram post, reply DM, sleep. where do I put YouTube?? YouTube is so much work — editing, thumbnail, description, SEO whatever. Fern can film but she cannot do everything

That's a very real concern and honestly the biggest reason most food creators burn out. YouTube is way more work than Instagram — a single well-produced video can take 5-8 hours between filming, editing, thumbnails, and optimizing the upload. And you're already running a full business plus managing your Instagram.

Have you thought about maybe starting with just one video to test it? Like a pilot episode? That way you can see how much time it actually takes without committing to a whole channel.

yes!! that is what I think too. maybe just try one video and see how it go. Fern say she can edit the first one for free because she want to build her portfolio also. so it would not cost me anything except time. but even time is expensive when you wake up at 4am haha

That's actually a great deal — free editing for the first video in exchange for portfolio work. That takes the biggest time sink off your plate for at least the trial run. The filming itself might only take an extra hour or two if Fern shoots while you're already doing your normal routine.

One video to test the waters seems like a low-risk move. Even if you decide YouTube isn't for you, you'd end up with a nice long-form piece you could clip for Instagram Reels too.

that is true!! I can use clips for Instagram na ka. actually my Instagram is 47K followers now so maybe some of them would come to YouTube too. but also maybe not because Instagram people are lazy and don't want to watch 10 minute video haha. different audience right??

You're actually right — there's some overlap but they are pretty different audiences. Instagram followers want quick, beautiful content they can scroll through. YouTube viewers are more willing to sit down and watch a full story. But your existing 47K is still a great launchpad to at least get some initial views on a first video.

The nice thing is, even if only 5-10% of your Instagram followers check out a YouTube video, that's 2,000-5,000 views on day one, which is a really strong start for a brand new channel.

hmm 2,000-5,000 view is not bad na ka!! but then what. I post one video and then what?? if I don't post regularly YouTube algorithm won't push it. and I cannot commit to like weekly video, I will die haha. maybe monthly?? or maybe I just don't do it. I keep going back and forth 😩

The algorithm pressure is real — YouTube does reward consistency, but plenty of food channels post monthly or even less and still grow because the content is evergreen. A khao man gai tutorial will get searched for years after you post it. It's not like trending content that dies in a week.

But honestly? You don't have to decide the whole strategy right now. You could just do the one video with Fern, see how it feels, see how it performs, and then decide. If it feels like a nightmare, you stop. If it's fun and gets good response, you figure out a pace that works. No need to commit to a schedule before you've even filmed one thing.

you are right na ka. I think too much. Fern say the same thing — just try!! but also she is not the one waking up at 4am every day haha. okay I will think about it more. maybe I talk to Fern this weekend and see what she think about timeline. or maybe I just focus on the cart because the cart is doing so good right now and I don't want to be distracted

That's also a totally valid perspective — your cart is clearly thriving and there's something to be said for staying focused on what's already working rather than spreading yourself thin. Not every opportunity has to be a yes, even if it sounds exciting.

Talk to Fern, sleep on it, and go with your gut. You know your energy levels and your limits better than anyone. Whether you do the YouTube thing or not, your khao man gai business is solid. There's no rush on this decision.

exactly na ka!! no rush. I will think about it. the cart always come first. thank you for talking it through with me!! sometimes I just need someone to listen while I think out loud haha. okay 4am alarm is coming, I need to sleep 🙏 goodnight!!

That's what I'm here for! Thinking out loud is totally valid — it helps to hear yourself say it. Whatever you decide about YouTube, you've got a good head on your shoulders and you'll make the right call.

Get some sleep before that 4am alarm. The market and the charcoal stove will be waiting! Goodnight! 🙏
